question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R3 shuts down GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1 (the R3↔R4 link, primary-path
metric 10). The IS-IS adjacency R3↔R4 drops; SPF recomputes with the
primary path removed. TI-LFA, which was pre-programmed on R2's
GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 to use the backup R2→R6→R7→R4 path, activates
for sub-50ms convergence — but afterwards the global SPF reconvergence
promotes the backup as the new primary (total metric 60 via R6+R7+R4).
Ideal test of TI-LFA end-to-end: detect the link drop, confirm FRR
pre-programmed backup activated, then verify post-SPF state.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on R3, GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1 (the R3<->R4 primary link, metric 10) was administratively shut down.
Shutting the interface tears down the R3<->R4 IS-IS adjacency, so that link can no longer carry the primary path; the pre-programmed TI-LFA backup on R2's Gi0/0/0/0 activates for sub-50ms protection, then SPF reconverges to promote the backup R2->R6->R7->R4 path (total metric 60) as the new primary. Reachability to R4 is preserved via the longer path.
Fix: no shutdown on R3's GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1.
expected_symptoms_summary:
Interface state change (%PKT_INFRA-LINK-5-CHANGED):
- R3: GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1 → Administratively Down.

revert.commands:
configure terminal
interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1
 no shutdown

expected_symptoms: RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:16:57.203 UTC: ifmgr[333]: %PKT_INFRA-LINK-5-CHANGED : Interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1, changed state to Administratively Down
